scope_boundary: synthetic proof-carrying claim record discipline and finite Lean record predicates only; no theorem validity, semantic equivalence, citation accuracy, verifier quality, open-domain formalization, source interpretation, runtime behavior, or source-derived support-state promotion
transition_reason: The repository now has a proof-carrying-claim schema, a protocol fixture, finite ProofCarryingClaims Lean predicates, proof adequacy review text that classifies the target as useful but too narrow, exact source mappings for the Spinoza chapter, and a synthetic proof-carrying claim harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures. Those artifacts make verifier artifact refs, tier/justification alignment, bounded review eligibility, failed-attempt preservation, formalization mismatch escalation, timeout/failure blocking, and non-claim boundaries executable at the record level. They still do not establish theorem validity, semantic equivalence between prose and formalization, citation accuracy, verifier quality, open-domain formalization, source interpretation adequacy, or a deployed verifier.
required_artifacts: real verifier-output trace with proof, citation, procedure, or tribunal artifacts
semantic-equivalence review connecting a prose claim to a formal or procedural representation
citation accuracy review for citation-dossier claims
formalization mismatch corpus with positive and negative controls
failed-attempt ledger showing downgrade, block, or tribunal routing across actual claims
independent review of verifier quality and source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_proof_carrying_claims.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_source_evidence_audit.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: schema validation checks record shape rather than theorem truth
finite Lean predicates remain useful but too narrow for semantic proof adequacy
semantic-equivalence review, citation accuracy review, verifier-quality review, formalization benchmarks, and deployed verifier traces are not implemented
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as theorem-validity evidence
narrow proof-carrying fixture interpreted as verifier-quality evidence
citation dossier fixture interpreted as citation accuracy evidence
formalization mismatch fixture treated as semantic-equivalence proof
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to the synthetic record discipline that passed, or after verifier-output traces, semantic-equivalence review, citation accuracy review, formalization mismatch tests, failed-attempt ledger behavior, verifier-quality review, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: synthetic fixtures do not run an open-domain verifier
schema validation does not prove theorem truth or citation accuracy
finite Lean predicates do not prove semantic adequacy of natural-language claims
formalization mismatch fixtures do not establish a general mismatch detector
failed-attempt refs in fixtures do not prove a deployed verifier ledger
no independent formal-methods, verifier-quality, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent formal-methods, verifier-quality, citation, semantic-equivalence, or source-interpretation audit
non_claims: does not promote Spinoza above argument
does not prove runtime behavior or open-domain formalization
